summary: Being a 'token factory' is sufficient as a business model for the next 5-10 years; the world needs more token factories
summary_extended: Baker argues that operating as a 'token factory' — a pure-play AI compute provider focused solely on generating tokens at scale — is a fully sufficient and durable business model for the next 5-10 years, without needing to build out a full cloud stack (databases, storage, orchestration layers, etc.) as AWS or Azure have done. His reasoning is structural: global demand for AI inference and training compute is so large and growing so fast that specialization in token generation alone is a defensible and highly valuable position. The investment implication is that pure-play AI compute providers and neoclouds should not be penalized for lacking broader cloud services, and that their focused model is a feature, not a limitation. Baker frames this as a contrarian pushback against the view that token factories must eventually verticalize to compete with hyperscalers.
detail: Baker explicitly states that being a token factory is 'more than enough for the next 5 to 10 years' and that the world needs more of them. This validates pure-play AI compute providers without needing to build full cloud stacks (databases, storage, etc.) like AWS.
confidence_evidence: Baker states this directly and emphatically as a structural view with a specific time horizon
quote_fragment: being a token factory is more than enough for the next 5 to 10 years

ticker_audit.reasoning: CoreWeave is the canonical public pure-play neocloud / token factory and is the most direct public equity expression of this thesis; Baker's structural view on token factories validates CoreWeave's business model explicitly.

More token factories means more GPU clusters being built and operated; NVIDIA is the dominant supplier of the compute hardware that token factories run, making it a clear and unambiguous direct beneficiary of this structural view.
